jobs.label: fix
jobs.prompt:
Add a provider health pre-check to the workflow dispatch pipeline so unreachable agents are caught before a run is submitted — not after jobs start failing.

Current behavior: AgentSpawner().preflight(agent_slug) is called at job execution time in Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/_execution_core.py lines ~292-303. If a provider is down, the job starts, then fails with setup_failure. This wastes a run slot and produces misleading failure records.

Fix: in WorkflowPipeline.pre_dispatch() (Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow_pipeline.py, around the posture/governance/loop-detection gates), add a preflight pass that:
1. Collects all unique agent slugs from the spec jobs
2. Calls AgentSpawner().preflight(slug) for each
3. If any are not ready, fail the gate before submission with a clear message naming which agents are unavailable

Skip preflight for 'human' routes and 'integration/*' routes — only check model-backed agents. Keep the check fast (don't wait on slow probes — use the existing readiness result, not a fresh probe).
